acceptance.proof: One caller-selected provider call returns all six transport-owned values with one witness; no mixed-provider composition, interface inspection, or ambient acquisition occurs.

Provider identity, epoch, and revision evidence is separately inspectable and must exactly match the caller's expected transport-owner witness; no clock, arrival-time, reachability, or authorization inference occurs.

V4Address, V4DataPort, V4ServicePort, V6Address, V6DataPort, then V6ServicePort are checked as opaque Unicode-scalar text against the existing O transport maximum with deterministic first-failure evidence and preserved accepted allocations.
Accepted state consumes into exactly six correctly labelled S provider values and neither constructs nor admits a complete volatile snapshot.

Feature/dependency closure remains empty and no interface inspection, address/port parsing, socket, network, reachability, authorization, device, activation, runtime effect, or Manifold authority surface opens.

commit_policy: Commit the six-value transport-owner acquisition/evidence lane separately from platform acquisition, T/U, complete S admission, activation, sockets, networking, devices, and authority.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the six-value transport provider, shared witness, opaque endpoint text, S separation, and ambient network-acquisition prohibition.
Describe the explicit transport-lane evidence adapter without claiming platform acquisition, endpoint semantics, networking, or activation.
Review shared owner-witness and transport-plane guidance; retain unchanged because artifact, authority, and external-operation separation already cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review installed skill without public mutation.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review Rusty LSL and Manifold routing; retain unchanged because no ownership or cross-repo routing changes.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review installed skill without public mutation.

non_scope: Modification of accepted O, S, T, or U source/evidence; Cargo manifests/locks; project/feature locks; dependencies; features; unsafe; native libraries; oracle drivers; external implementation inputs.

Interface enumeration or inspection, address or port parsing/validation/normalization, DNS, socket creation or binding, network reads/writes, reachability checks, authorization, randomness, process spawning, provider discovery/selection, retry/background work, or platform adapters.

Implementation/runtime lane acquisition, complete LSLC-001S snapshot construction/admission, XML/document behavior, protocol, wire, discovery, inlet, outlet, buffering, recovery, FFI, runtime activation, devices, packages, permissions, or effects.
Manifold admission, identity, registry, subscription, route, lease, revision, replay, authorization, audit, session, topology, transport, provider epoch, command, product, or network authority.
objective: Add one dependency-free explicitly invoked adapter contract that acquires v4address, v4data_port, v4service_port, v6address, v6data_port, and v6service_port together from one caller-selected transport-owner provider, retains one separately inspectable shared owner identity/epoch/revision witness, requires exact expected-witness match, validates the six opaque values in LSLC-001O order, and projects exactly the LSLC-001S transport lane without complete snapshot admission or ambient platform/network acquisition.
outputs: One synchronous caller-selected provider trait returning six opaque transport-owned values and one shared transport-owner witness in one call.
Bounded owner identity plus exact owner-issued epoch/revision evidence retained separately from the six values.
Typed provider, witness, and fixed-order value-bound failures with accepted allocation preservation.
A consuming projection to exactly V4Address, V4DataPort, V4ServicePort, V6Address, V6DataPort, and V6ServicePort LSLC-001S values without invoking complete admission.
